page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for its hardware class. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a stress-ng single-core score of 1577.49 and a multi-core score of 16666.15. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 932.17 GB/sec, which drops to 88.33 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a write bandwidth of 51.77 GB/sec. The system supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 vector and deep learning boost extensions, which assist in cryptographic and vector processing tasks, as reflected in its Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 14814.7.

Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.hfr7.4xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching systems, and enterprise applications. This is supported by its Passmark database operations score of 6071.77 and a Redis SET performance of 16.05 million operations per second. The instance also handles web serving efficiently, achieving a static web serving throughput of 13.8 GB/sec.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-CPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark results show solid performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 16666.15 and a Redis SET throughput of 16.05 million operations per second. Supported by AVX-512 extensions, this instance offers a balanced, cost-effective profile for memory-intensive applications. It is highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and static web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized dedicated instance featuring high memory-to-core density and advanced vector extensions for demanding database and caching workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core

Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
